feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: NBA Draft: BYU forward AJ Dybantsa selected No. 1 overall by Wizards
entry.content: Dybantsa possesses an elite combination of size, athleticism, skill and drive to develop into a multi-time All-NBA selection.

entry.title: Why Becky Hammon won't apologize for her Jalen Brunson championship take
entry.content: The Knicks point guard proved many wrong with his title, but one vocal critic isn't apologizing for doubting him.

entry.title: Chicago White Sox host players with special needs at The Rate
entry.content: The White Sox are tied for first place and have an important game ahead, but for many players, an earlier event at the ballpark carried even greater significance.

entry.title: Dream job: US soccer fans paid to watch every World Cup game
entry.content: It might just be the greatest job in the world.Akoto quit his job and broke up with his partner to take up the "chief World Cup watcher" role, meant to generate excitement around the once-every-four-years event being hosted by the US, Canada and Mexico.

entry.title: England frustrated by Ghana in dour World Cup draw
entry.content: England must wait to secure qualification for the World Cup knockout phase as they are held to a draw by Ghana at Boston Stadium.

entry.title: Christian Watson: Packers focused on finishing after 2025 collapse
entry.content: The Packers lost four consecutive games to close out the regular season.

entry.title: Cristiano Ronaldo matches Lionel Messi record with goal in Portugal vs Uzbekistan
entry.content: Cristiano Ronaldo finally ended his 10-game scoring drought during Portugal vs. Uzbekistan World Cup matchup.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
A man cannot be comfortable without his own approval.
Lucky Numbers: 28, 48, 7, 38, 80, 57

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Get into a routine of stretching your hip flexors and chest if you sit for long periods.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: It's impossible to sneeze with your eyes open.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Samuel Beckett
entry.content: “You're on earth. There's no cure for that.” — Samuel Beckett

entry.title: Which players failed to make an impact? England player ratings
entry.content: BBC Sport England reporter Alex Howell rates the players after the 0-0 draw with Ghana - plus have your say.

entry.title: Ex-Red Wing Mike Babcock returns to NHL as Oilers' coach
entry.content: Mike Babcock led the Red Wings to the Stanley Cup in 2008. Now 63, he'll try and lead Connor McDavid and the Oilers to the Cup.

entry.title: Injured Marcelo Mayer Out Of Red Sox Lineup, Walking With Limp
entry.content: Boston Red Sox shortstop Marcelo Mayer is injured.

entry.title: Croatia's Luka Modric joins Ronaldo and Messi in historic club
entry.content: 40-year-old Luka Modric becomes just fourth man with 200 international appearances.

entry.title: Dusty May: 'It wasn't an easy decision' to leave Michigan for Mavericks
entry.content: The Dallas Mavericks announced their hiring of Dusty May on Tuesday. Here's what the ex-Michigan coach had to say on his departure.

entry.title: World Cup announcer stripped of FIFA credential over Almirón red-card tirade
entry.content: A Paraguayan football announcer has had his credentials revoked after launching into a wild tirade over a red card issued against Paraguay in their match against Türkiye on Friday. The inciting incident occurred in the first half during a play stoppage. Paraguay’s Miguel Almirón appeared to say something to Türkiye’s Mert Müldür while covering his…

entry.title: Wayne Dowdle elected AutoZone Liberty Bowl Festival Association president
entry.content: Wayne Dowdle has been elected president of the 2026 AutoZone Liberty Bowl Festival Association.

entry.title: 2026 NBA Rookie-Scale Contracts and Salaries: How Much Does Each First-Round Pick Get Paid?
entry.content: The 2026 NBA Draft gets underway tonight at the Barclays Center in Brooklyn, New York, and as always, the spotlight is on the players expected to hear their names called first. Prospects like AJ Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson, Cameron Boozer, and Caleb Wilson are expected to be the top selections, but how much will they earn on their rookie contracts? There’s a major difference in pay depending on how early a player hears his name called, so here’s a simple breakdown of how the NBA’s rookie-scale con

entry.title: Arizona Cardinals training camp public practice schedule announced
entry.content: The Arizona Cardinals will hold seven training camp practices open to the public from July 24-Aug. 9.

entry.title: Most IVF ‘add-on’ treatments have no effect on fertility or remain unproven, study says
entry.content: Review shows majority of procedures offered alongside standard IVF not backed by reliable evidence Most IVF “add-on” treatments sold to people hoping to boost their chances of having children are not backed by reliable evidence, fail to boost fertility and may be a complete waste of money, the largest study of its kind has concluded.There has been a surge in extra procedures, medicines or techniques offered to patients in addition to standard IVF with bold claims they will increase the probability of success. Take-up is widespread, with more than 70% of IVF patients in the UK, Australia and New Zealand paying for one or more add-on during IVF treatment.Acupuncture – inserting thin needles into points on the body.Corticosteroids – medication to reduce inflammation and suppress immune system activity.Endometrial receptivity testing – a biopsy of the uterine lining to assess gene expression patterns.Intralipid infusion – a liquid containing fats that is administered into the blood.Intraovarian injection of platelet-rich plasma – injecting platelet-rich plasma into the ovaries.Intrauterine infusion of platelet-rich plasma – insertion of platelet-rich plasma into the uterus.Pre-implantation genetic testing for aneuploidy – a screening test to check whether embryos have the expected number of chromosomes.EmbryoGlue – an embryo transfer medium containing hyaluronic acid. The evidence review found it may increase the probability of pregnancy and live birth; however, the effect on live birthrates was not robust.Endometrial scratching – a minor procedure undertaken to scratch or disturb the lining of the uterus. The review found this may increase the probability of pregnancy and live birth.Physiological intracytoplasmic sperm injection (PICSI) – a technique used to select sperm based on their ability to bind to hyaluronic acid. There was weak evidence this may lower the risk of miscarriage. Continue reading...

entry.title: High school senior gets over 5 years in prison for setting homeless man on fire on NYC subway
entry.content: A high school senior who admitted to setting a fire that severely burned a homeless man on the subway was sentenced Tuesday in Manhattan federal court to 5 1/2 years in prison.

entry.title: California intends to sue Trump administration over deal to end offshore wind project
entry.content: California intends to sue the Trump administration over its deal to end an offshore wind project proposed off the state's central coast.

entry.title: Endangered pregnant whale found dead on bow of Royal Caribbean cruise ship in Alaska
entry.content: A cruise ship struck a pregnant fin whale in waters off the coast of Alaska, killing the endangered marine mammal.

entry.title: Aston Villa make Morgan Rogers stance crystal clear as Man United lurk
entry.content: Aston Villa’s position on a potential sale of Morgan Rogers has emerged, even as the likes of Manchester United weigh up a swoop for the player.Rogers interestRogers is a man in great demand this su...

entry.title: Why three-star LB Cade Haug decommitted from Texas
entry.content: The consensus three-star prospect pledged to the Horns in February.

entry.title: Why Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo and Kylian Mbappe World Cup records aren’t that impressive
entry.content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is setting the stage for a Golden Boot race for the ages as all-time records fall like dominoes – and we have not yet reached the end of the group stages – but it all just feels a bit meh. Lionel Messi currently leads the way with five goals from his first two games for Argentina, while Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland sit just behind with four goals each.

entry.title: How Capitals trading for Jordan Kyrou relates to Alex Ovechkin retirement decision
entry.content: Washington has added a talented forward to its ranks.

entry.title: Wenceel Pérez returns home, but when will he return to Detroit Tigers?
entry.content: Wenceel Pérez is back in Detroit and "feeling better" after an orbital fracture in Houston, but he's not likely to return to the Detroit Tigers soon.

entry.title: Wenceel Pérez returns home, but when will he return to Detroit Tigers?
entry.content: Wenceel Pérez is back in Detroit and "feeling better" after an orbital fracture in Houston, but he's not likely to return to the Detroit Tigers soon.

entry.title: 17 states and trade group sue California over plastics packaging law
entry.content: A coalition of 17 states and a trade association representing U.S. wholesalers and distributors have sued California to block the enforcement of a stringent recycling law that aims to reduce plastic packaging waste.

entry.title: 6 convictions found to be fair after review of embattled Minnesota medical examiner's testimony
entry.content: A Minnesota county prosecutor said Tuesday that convictions in six different murder cases were fair despite the involvement of an embattled medical examiner whose work was called into question after a federal judge described his testimony in one high-profile case as "unreliable, misleading and inaccurate."

entry.title: N.Y. school board vice president arrested on child pornography, endangerment charges
entry.content: Travis Barr Longo, who described himself as the first drag performer elected to a public school board, resigned Monday after facing federal and state charges for child endangerment and receiving and possessing child pornography. He was the vice president of the Cazenovia Central School District Board of Education in central New York.

entry.title: Federal officials plan to offload some warehouses purchased for immigrant detention
entry.content: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement is retreating from a plan to use warehouses to hold up to 10,000 people on a single site, jettisoning a key piece of former Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem's $38-billion plan to rapidly expand detention capacity this year.

entry.title: Spread of abortion pills casts pall over Dobbs anniversary for pro-life side
entry.content: For pro-life advocates, the fourth anniversary of the Dobbs decision offers a grim reminder that while they won the battle to overturn Roe v. Wade, they're losing the war.

entry.title: Tech Stocks Retreat on Concerns over AI Spending, Valuations
entry.content: Plus, SpaceX shares snap losing streak and Oracle puts a number on layoffs.

entry.title: Protesters Receive Decadeslong Sentences for Texas ICE Facility Clash
entry.content: A man who shot a police officer gets 100 years in prison, while other sentences range from 30 to 70 years.

entry.title: Russia Pressures Belarus in Bid to Open New Front in Ukraine War
entry.content: With Moscow’s prospects faltering on the battlefield, the Kremlin is trying to nudge its closest ally off the sidelines.

entry.title: Texas Senate hopeful Talarico under fire over resurfaced 'Christian who hates Christianity' remarks
entry.content: In resurfaced audio from 2021, Texas Democratic U.S. Senate hopeful JAMES TALARICO describes himself as 'a Christian who hates Christianity.'

entry.title: Dallas Mavericks draft picks tracker 2026: Live results, grades, updates, list of selections and trades
entry.content: The Mavs had the top pick a year ago and took Cooper Flagg.

entry.title: Brewers sign 3.44 ERA right-hander after short Yankees tenure
entry.content: Milwaukee is familiar with the 31-year-old pitcher who was recently with the Yankees.

entry.title: Red Sox sign former Cardinals, Giants, Rangers 31-year-old catcher
entry.content: Boston is adding to their catching depth chart this summer.

entry.title: Trump administration charges 455 people, including doctors, with $6.5 billion in healthcare fraud
entry.content: Trump administration charges 455 people, including doctors, with $6.5 billion in healthcare fraud

entry.title: Why the NFL took a stand on Brendan Sorsby, 2026 supplemental draft
entry.content: Sorsby isn't headed to the league just yet.

entry.title: Stunning Celtics trade proposal would cut ties with Jaylen Brown for $149 million Finals MVP
entry.content: The Boston Celtics are listening to trade offers on forward Jaylen Brown, and a stunning trade proposal from an NBA analyst would send Brown to the Western Conference for a two-time Finals MVP.

entry.title: Novig Promo Code TSNEWS50: Get $50 Trade Bonus for England-Ghana, World Cup Predictions
entry.content: New customers can sign up with the Novig promo code TSNEWS50 and grab a $50 bonus for World Cup predictions.

entry.title: NBA Draft picks tracker 2026: Live results, updates, list of selections from Rounds 1-2
entry.content: The Sporting News is tracking live results for every pick in the 2026 NBA Draft, from the start of Round 1 through No. 60.

entry.title: AI stock slump raises question if investors are taking profits or getting nervous
entry.content: Tech companies are spending big on AI, but investors are getting nervous

entry.title: Here's why cables for Thunderbolt 4 and 5 ports cost so much
entry.content: Thunderbolt looks like USB-C, but there's a lot more inside.

entry.title: Georgia’s QR codes for counting votes will remain for midterms after lawmakers vote to delay a fix
entry.content: Georgia will stick with an embattled vote-counting method that relies on QR codes for this year’s midterm elections after state lawmakers passed legislation that put off making changes until 2028

entry.title: Georgia's QR codes for counting votes will remain for midterms after lawmakers vote to delay a fix
entry.content: Georgia will stick with an embattled vote-counting method that relies on a QR code for this year's midterm elections after lawmakers passed legislation Tuesday that put off making changes until 2028.

entry.title: Judge says food stamps can be used to buy candy, soda
entry.content: The Trump administration cannot bar using food stamps to buy candy and sugary drinks, a federal judge ruled Monday.

entry.title: Supreme Court sides with Michigan county in a tax foreclosure case
entry.content: The Supreme Court on Tuesday rejected an effort to change tax foreclosure sales to let homeowners to keep more money when their property is sold to recoup unpaid taxes.

entry.title: Meta reportedly dips its pathetic toes into the prediction market space
entry.content: Mark Zuckerberg has reportedly directed Meta to build its own prediction market.

entry.title: Sweet and spicy is the flavor combo everyone is obsessed with right now
entry.content: Sweet heat is everywhere. This isn't a passing phase; it's one of the biggest flavor shifts in years, and home cooks are fully in on it.

entry.title: Trump says 6 people have been arrested for damaging the Reflecting Pool
entry.content: President Donald Trump says six people have been arrested for damaging the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, which Trump recently ordered to be painted what he calls “American Flag Blue.”

entry.title: $75 caviar-topped tots. A day's pay worth of beer. Here's the World Cup menu -- and prices
entry.content: World Cup tickets are expensive. Flights to North America are expensive. Hotel rooms in many places are expensive.

entry.title: Supreme Court rules against Louisiana prisoner's lawsuit over shaved dreadlocks
entry.content: The Supreme Court ruled Tuesday that a Rastafarian inmate cannot sue the Louisiana prison officials who shaved his head, which he argued violated his religious rights.

entry.title: Netflix's new horror game taps into your phone for deeper immersion
entry.content: Netflix's new horror game uses your smartphone to control stars Zoë Kravitz's hands.

entry.title: Sorry, Slackbot. Claude is taking your job
entry.content: Anthropic has introduced @Claude for Slack.

entry.title: Justice Department withdraws subpoenas that sought reporters' grand jury testimony, sources say
entry.content: The Justice Department issued and then withdrew subpoenas that sought to compel reporters at The Washington Post and The Wall Street Journal to testify before a grand jury, according to people familiar with the matter.

entry.title: 8 convicted of terrorism charges in Texas immigration center shooting sentenced to decades in prison
entry.content: A demonstrator who shot and wounded a police officer outside a Texas immigration center last July 4 was sentenced to 100 years in federal prison Tuesday, while other protesters accused of having links to antifa were given multiple decades in federal prison.

entry.title: Is Cristiano Ronaldo playing today? Latest news on Portugal lineup for 2026 World Cup game vs. Uzbekistan
entry.content: Portugal returns to the pitch on Tuesday afternoon for a pivotal Group K clash with Uzbekistan, with its 2026 World Cup hopes in need of a jolt.

entry.title: New York Yankees manager reveals Anthony Volpe status before Tigers game
entry.content: Is Anthony Volpe playing tonight against the Detroit Tigers?

entry.title: Portugal vs. Uzbekistan box score: Full stats from World Cup Group K match, including Cristiano Ronaldo
entry.content: Portugal will be hoping to get back on track in its second match.

entry.title: Nationals get bad injury update on 4.16 ERA left-hander
entry.content: This is a tough break for the Nationals' 25-year-old.

entry.title: Phillies prospect Dante Nori's dad hired by NBA's Trail Blazers as their new head coach
entry.content: This is a pretty fun MLB and NBA crossover storyline.

entry.title: Portugal vs. Uzbekistan live score, result, highlights from 2026 World Cup Group K match
entry.content: The Sporting News is tracking live scoring updates and highlights for Portugal vs. Uzbekistan in the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Follow for complete results from the Group K match in Houston.

entry.title: Portugal vs. Uzbekistan lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 Group K game in Houston
entry.content: Portugal hopes it can rescue its 2026 FIFA World Cup trajectory as the European giants take on Uzbekistan Tuesday from NRG Stadium in Houston.

entry.title: How worried should we be about bird flu? – podcast
entry.content: Having wiped out millions of birds worldwide and devastated wildlife populations around the globe, Australia has watched as the H5N1 strain of bird flu crept ever closer.And now it’s here, on the last continent to be hit. Birds have been discovered on beaches in Western Australia and tested positive for the virus.Reged Ahmad speaks to Graham Readfearn about how governments and wildlife experts alike are anxiously waiting to see if this is just the beginningRead more: Continue reading...

entry.title: Supreme Court sides with Michigan county in a tax foreclosure case
entry.content: The Supreme Court has rejected an effort to reshape tax foreclosure sales to allow the original owners to keep more money when homes are sold to recoup unpaid taxes

entry.title: Which debts are forgiven when someone dies?
entry.content: When a loved one dies, certain types of debts may disappear, but others may impact heirs and the estate.

entry.title: Opella Growth Chief on Using AI to Combat Health Misinformation
entry.content: Opella Growth Chief on Using AI to Combat Health Misinformation

entry.title: Jim Parsons says he knew he wanted to be part of "Titaníque" when offered role of Rose's mom
entry.content: Jim Parsons speaks about starring in the musical "Titaníque," which is a comedic retelling of the story of the "Titanic," from the perspective and songs of Celine Dion. Parsons talks about why he wanted to portray's Rose's mother, the show's impact on the LGBTQ+ community and the cast.

entry.title: 40 drowning deaths reported in France as Europe swelters in heat wave
entry.content: Millions in France are enduring extreme heat, with temperatures soaring and 40 drowning deaths reported since June 18.

entry.title: Supreme Court rules ex-inmate can't sue prison officials for shaving dreadlocks
entry.content: The Supreme Court rejected a former Louisiana inmate's effort to sue state prison officials after they shaved his dreadlocks in violation of his religious beliefs.

entry.title: Andy Burnham rises as top pick to succeed Keir Starmer as prime minister
entry.content: British lawmaker Andy Burnham is a favorite pick to replace Prime Minister Keir Starmer following Starmer's resignation announcement. Author and journalist Ian Dunt has more context.

entry.title: Trump says Iran agreed to nuclear inspections, but Tehran denies claim
entry.content: Iran insists there are no plans for inspections of its bombed nuclear sites, but Trump says Tehran "fully and completely" agreed to let inspectors return.

entry.title: Trump blames vandals for Reflecting Pool problems without providing proof
entry.content: Preparations are underway to drain the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington, D.C., for repairs after a multi-million dollar renovation gone awry. President Trump is blaming vandals for an alleged 350-foot gash in the pool's blue lining. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe has more on this.

entry.title: Meta debuts new, cheaper smart glasses under its own brand
entry.content: The smart glasses are available in several countries starting today in a variety of color and lens combinations.

entry.title: Tech stocks tumble for a second day. Here's what is behind the selloff.
entry.content: Nvidia, Alphabet and other technology stocks fell as Wall Street shifted from rewarding AI spending to demanding evidence that it will produce outsized returns.

entry.title: 4 days left to save up to $190 on TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026
entry.content: Four days left to save up to $190 on your pass to TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026 - the ultimate founder bootcamp - before Early Bird rates end on June 26 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register here.

entry.title: Ribbie turns real-time baseball stats into arcade-like, pixel-art broadcasts
entry.content: Ribbie lets you follow along live with MLB games with a delightful, arcade-inspired interface.

entry.title: ‘I’d pause then carry on’: Peter Marinker, star of Krapp’s Last Tape, on performing with Alzheimer’s
entry.content: The 84-year-old actor has spent decades bringing Samuel Beckett’s plays to life. Does his recent diagnosis give him new insights into playing ‘sad clown’ Krapp in a drama about age and the battlefield of memory?What a lot of Krapp. Pardon my French but Samuel Beckett’s haunting 1958 masterpiece about regret and isolation is having a moment. Stephen Rea recently took Krapp’s Last Tape on an international tour, Gary Oldman returned to the stage after decades away to deliver the tragicomic one-man show and this summer Stockard Channing will direct it at the Edinburgh fringe, with David Westhead as Krapp. Beckett’s eponymous loner, who sits in his dark den and ritually listens to tapes he made as a younger man, is riding a new wave of popularity.Peter Marinker first played Krapp half a lifetime ago and is preparing to star in a new production, reusing the tapes he recorded in 1983. How does he feel listening back now? “I thought of redoing them – it could have been better,” he says when we meet at the tiny Cockpit theatre in London. That assessment matches the spirit of the self-lacerating Krapp who looks back not just in anger but anguish. Marinker quotes Dennis Potter, who said we should consider our past with “tender contempt”. He adds wryly: “That rang a bell.” Continue reading...

entry.title: A shooter kills 2 at a Northern California library and an 18-year-old suspect is arrested
entry.content: Police investigating a shooting at a library in Northern California that left two people dead say an 18-year-old suspect has been arrested

entry.title: New Trump book’s authors detail how they pried loose White House secrets: ‘We nearly killed ourselves’
entry.content: Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan, the reporters behind Regime Change, were up against an administration that is ‘very good at keeping secrets’They cracked the White House situation room, unearthing secrets from the heart of a secretive administration. But the reporters behind Regime Change, a blockbuster new book on Donald Trump’s second term, ran up against a wall when reporting on one issue surrounding the 80-year-old US president: his fitness for office.“His health has always been a very specific lockbox for him, going back decades,” Maggie Haberman, co-author with Jonathan Swan, said in an interview. “Illness freaks him out; he perceives illness as weakness, usually, and he certainly perceives any sense that he is having an issue as a projection of weakness, and his advisers are very, very attuned to that. Continue reading...

entry.title: Mike Penders set to compete for a first-team role at Chelsea after Strasbourg loan
entry.content: Following a successful loan spell with Strasbourg, Mike Penders (20) is set to return to Stamford Bridge and compete for a first-team role next season, according to The Athletic. The Belgian youth int...

entry.title: Team: Evenepoel and Lipowitz as Red Bull joint Tour leaders can work
entry.content: Red Bull-Bora–Hansgrohe team boss Ralph Denk does not see any potential for conflict after naming German cycling star Florian Lipowitz and Belgian Olympic champion Remco Evenepoel as joint team leaders for July's Tour de France. "I am convinced that this will work very well," Denk told a media round on Tuesday ahead of the Tour, which starts on July 4 in Barcelona, adding: "Remco is still the front man and Florian is a bit of the wingman.

entry.title: Is there a sell-on clause for Anderson?
entry.content: As far as I'm aware Newcastle would not stand to benefit financially if Anderson left Nottingham Forest. Ordinarily these clauses would be negotiated during the sale of promising young players, as sides like Manchester City and Chelsea have over the years.

entry.title: Eduardo Camavinga has already told ‘intelligent’ Liverpool target what he needs to do
entry.content: Eduardo Camavinga has long been linked with a Liverpool move.The Frenchman was on the Reds' shortlist before he chose to join Real Madrid back in 2021 and, since then, he's not too far out of Liverpoo...

entry.title: William Gomes: Porto slash asking price for Man United target
entry.content: Manchester United have reportedly been handed a major boost in their pursuit of William Gomes, despite two new clubs entering an already red-hot race for the Brazilian winger.Wing WizardGomes has emer...

entry.title: Former 8th overall pick in MLB Draft announces sudden retirement from baseball at age 23
entry.content: Just a few years ago, Benny Montgomery looked like one of the brightest young prospects in baseball. Now, at only 23 years old, his professional baseball journey has come to an unexpected end. Montgomery, the eighth overall selection in the…

entry.title: N.J.’s World Cup party was such a drag. Thanks, Norway, for rowing to our rescue
entry.content: The rest of the continent seems to be having a better time at the World Cup than we are in New Jersey.

entry.title: “Makes the right decision” Ollie Watkins heaps praise on Arsenal man

entry.title: Oil prices fall as investors focus on Hormuz flows after peace talks
entry.content: Oil prices fall as investors focus on Hormuz flows after peace talks

entry.title: ACLU to monitor election certification as part of $50 million midterm effort
entry.content: Half of the group’s spending will go toward ensuring the proper administration of elections in key states amid Trump’s efforts to exert greater influence over the process.

entry.title: Trouble getting weight loss drugs covered by insurance? Here's what to know
entry.content: If your doctor prescribes a GLP-1 medication for weight loss but your insurance won't cover it, you have options.

entry.title: Zohran Mamdani’s clout, a double Trump endorsement and more on the line in Tuesday’s primaries
entry.content: The parties will also set up a handful of key House battleground races as voters in Maryland, New York, South Carolina and Utah pick nominees.
